title: LLM Observability, Evaluation, Experimentation Platform — Dat Ngo, Arize
summary: Arize Phoenix is an open-source LLM observability, evaluation, and experimentation platform that runs as a single container without requiring Kubernetes. The enterprise version includes Alex, an AI layer that automatically scans traces, surfaces latency and errors, and generates evaluations to reduce manual observability work.
writeup: This talk from Arize covers Phoenix, an open-source platform for LLM observability, evaluation, and experimentation that runs as a single container and does not require Kubernetes. It walks through core practices including tracing, evaluation approaches such as LLM as judge and deterministic checks, golden datasets, and tying observability to business metrics. The enterprise version adds Alex, an AI layer that scans traces automatically, surfaces latency and errors, and generates evaluations to cut manual work. The takeaway is a path from raw traces to evaluations with reduced operational setup.
